title: The Rewle of Sustris Menouresses Enclosid with Appendix (rule-and-observance compilation)
summary: The repository artifact is a pinned CME-submodule representation of University of Michigan item RuleMinoresses, produced in 1999 by scanning and ScanWorx OCR from Walter W. Seton's contribution to EETS Original Series 148 (1914). Seton's edition transcribes Bodleian Library MS. Bodl. 585 Part II: the Middle English Isabella Rule at fols. 48r-72r, an unidentified papal document at fols. 72v-78v, and convent-observance regulations at fols. 78v-101r. The current shelfmark belongs to a composite codex whose unrelated Part I occupies fols. 1-47. The U-M selection retains the component title page and text at printed pp. 81-116 but omits Seton's introduction, bibliography, editorial note, single fol. 48r plate, endnotes, and glossary. A complete public scan of the 1914 book exists; only the printed fol. 48r plate is a public manuscript image, and no complete public Bodley 585 surrogate was found in current official catalog and Digital Bodleian searches.
review_notes: The cataloged unit is a three-text compilation: the main rule, Appendix papal document, and Appendix observance customary. Same-hand contiguity does not prove one author or one source document.
MS. Bodl. 585 is a current composite codex; only Part II is the source witness. Part I's St Albans material and Sir George More provenance are unrelated to RuleMinoresses.

The XML's 'olim Seton, olim Pennant' note belongs to sibling 3rdFranRule, whose witness is now NLW MS 22873B. RuleMinoresses derives from Bodley 585, as its own title page, Seton's edition, and the current Bodleian catalog agree.
Seton's public fol. 48r plate is partial manuscript-facsimile coverage inside a complete edition scan; it is not a complete Bodley 585 surrogate.
U-M's current No Copyright-United States statement and the restrictive 1999 XML availability notice are preserved as separate rights and provider-terms layers.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for RuleMinoresses
entities.description: Repository XML supplied through the pinned CME submodule.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item RuleMinoresses
entities.description: A 1999 electronic text created at the University of Michigan Humanities Text Initiative by scanning Seton's printed component and performing OCR with ScanWorx. It encodes the rule and both Appendix components with selected edition framing, not the whole 1914 volume or whole manuscript.
notes: The XML language declaration names Latin and Greek but omits the primary Middle English.
The text preserves manuscript leaf-side milestones 48r-101r and print-page milestones 81-116.
All twenty-seven Chapter and Section labels are SUPPLIED analytic headings.
Print footnote information is generally represented inline with CORR, DEL, UNCLEAR, and SUPPLIED; eight NOTE1 elements remain.

entities.label: Seton 1914 edition of two fifteenth-century Franciscan rules
alternate_labels: A Fifteenth-Century Courtesy Book and Two Fifteenth-Century Franciscan Rules
entities.description: Walter W. Seton's portion of a composite EETS volume. The Minoresses component comprises an introduction and bibliography (pp. 61-78), component title and editorial framing, one printed facsimile of Bodley 585 fol. 48r, the rule and Appendix (pp. 81-116), notes (pp. 117-125), and a glossary shared with the Third Order component (pp. 126-127).

bibliographic.citation: A Fifteenth-Century Courtesy Book, ed. R. W. Chambers, and Two Fifteenth-Century Franciscan Rules, ed. Walter W. Seton, Early English Text Society, Original Series 148 (London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co., and Humphrey Milford, Oxford University Press, 1914).
bibliographic.title: A Fifteenth-Century Courtesy Book and Two Fifteenth-Century Franciscan Rules
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 148
bibliographic.publisher: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co., and Humphrey Milford, Oxford University Press, for the Early English Text Society

notes: Chambers edits only the opening courtesy-book work; Seton edits both Franciscan components. Seton credits Mrs. Geoffrey Tomes with the Minoresses manuscript transcript and Chambers with helping revise that text.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Bodl. 585 (current composite codex)
entities.description: The current parchment codex unites two independently produced and donated volumes: unrelated Latin St Albans material at fols. i, 1-47 and the Middle English Minoresses unit at fols. 48-103. They were separately donated in 1604 and bound together after that date.

date_statements.label: current composite binding
date_statements.value: After the two separate donations of 1604

holding.notes: The holding-level shelfmark identifies the current composite host; Part II is separately modeled as the source witness.

physical_description.extent: i + 104 leaves; cataloged composite structure fols. i, 1-47 || fols. 48-103
notes: The catalog's total leaf count, physical part endpoint 103, and Part II msItem inferred endpoint 104 are retained as distinct statements pending direct inspection.
Part I's St Albans origin and Sir George More donation do not apply to the Minoresses unit.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Bodl. 585, Part II
alternate_labels: Rule of St. Clare manuscript unit
entities.description: The independently produced Middle English parchment unit of the current composite codex. It contains the rule at fols. 48r-72r, a papal-document fragment at fols. 72v-78v, and observance regulations at fols. 78v-101r, all copied in the same hand according to Seton.

date_statements.label: manuscript production
date_statements.value: Middle of the fifteenth century, c. 1440-1460

holding.notes: The part-level identifier is catalog-internal; researchers order the current shelfmark and specify Part II and target folios.

physical_description.extent: Current codicological unit cataloged as fols. 48-103; its single msItem has inferred locus 48-104; the edited compilation ends at fol. 101r
physical_description.format: Separately produced manuscript codex unit now bound in a composite volume
notes: Seton describes a neat and legible English book hand, finely decorated chapter initials, otherwise black writing, and the same hand through fol. 101r.
The current catalog records parchment and penwork and assigns English origin.
Bodleian/MLGB3 tentatively associates the unit with the London Abbey of St Mary the Virgin and St Francis outside Aldgate on evidence of contents; the question mark is substantive.
Presented by Charles Howard, earl of Nottingham, in 1604.
No historical Pennant or Seton designation applies to this witness.

entities.label: Seton 1914 printed facsimile of MS. Bodl. 585 fol. 48r
entities.description: One monochrome printed plate reproducing the opening manuscript page within EETS OS 148; it is not a complete manuscript surrogate and contains none of the remaining rule or Appendix folios.
entities.reproduction_format: One printed monochrome manuscript plate, subsequently visible in digital scans of the edition

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of EETS Original Series 148
entities.description: A complete 150-page digital scan of the composite 1914 volume from the University of Toronto Kelly Library copy. It contains the full printed Minoresses component and Seton's one fol. 48r plate, not a complete Bodley manuscript facsimile.

date_statements.label: digital availability
date_statements.value: Date not established in reviewed item metadata

entities.reproduction_format: PDF, OCR text, EPUB, and page-image derivatives

entities.label: Bodleian Western Medieval Manuscripts catalog record for MS. Bodl. 585
entities.description: The current official record, encoded in 2019 from the Summary Catalogue and A. G. Watson with later corrections and a 2021 MLGB3 provenance update. The exact TEI source is pinned and cached for reproducibility.

date_statements.label: record revision used
date_statements.value: 2019 description with provenance update of 14 December 2021, pinned at repository state reviewed 15 July 2026

entities.label: Isabella Rule as revised under Boniface VIII
entities.description: Source-work context for the main rule only: Urban IV confirmed the Longchamp or Isabella Rule in Religionis augmentum on 27 July 1263; the Middle English witness represents a later revision under Boniface VIII. Seton judges that the English translation probably passed through an unidentified French version.

date_statements.label: Boniface VIII revision
date_statements.value: During or attributed to the pontificate of Boniface VIII; exact date not established

notes: This entity does not represent a surviving physical source exemplar and does not apply to the two Appendix documents.

entities.label: U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright
entities.description: The April 2026 revision of the Copyright Office's duration guidance, used only to support the United States rights assessment for the 1914 edition; it is not part of the textual transmission lineage.

agents.name: R. W. Chambers
notes: Editor of the courtesy-book component, not of RuleMinoresses; Seton acknowledges his help revising the Minoresses text.

agents.name: Mrs. Geoffrey Tomes
notes: Named by Seton as the maker of the manuscript transcript; a fuller personal name was not established in reviewed sources.

assertion.notes: The header, identifier, title, structure, production statement, and current item page identify the U-M source; a current downloadable U-M source XML was not separately checksummed.

subject.description: Electronic component title page, seventeen-division rule, ten-section Appendix, and trailer

object.description: Seton's component title page and printed rule-plus-Appendix text

scope.notes: The encoding omits Seton's introduction, bibliography, editorial note, fol. 48r plate, endnotes, and shared glossary.

object.description: Rule, papal document, and observance regulations

scope.notes: Mrs. Geoffrey Tomes prepared the manuscript transcript; Seton edited it. The relation does not include unrelated Part I or unidentified material after fol. 101r.

object.description: Isabella Rule confirmed by Urban IV and revised under Boniface VIII

scope.notes: The relation identifies the translated source-work tradition; it neither identifies a physical Latin or French exemplar nor applies to the Appendix.

subject.description: Second codicological unit of the current composite host
locators: fols. 48-103 in the physical composite description

scope.notes: The two units were separately donated in 1604 and united after that date.

object.description: Current composite codex, including separate Part I and Part II descriptions

scope.notes: The plate does not reproduce fol. 48v or any remaining rule or Appendix page.
Completeness is edition-level. The scan's manuscript-facsimile coverage for Bodley 585 remains one page, fol. 48r.

notes: The catalog describes the work and witness but does not contain a transcription or manuscript page image.

access.format: HTML catalog description and TEI XML source

access.access_method: Exact Digital Bodleian API searches by shelfmark, title, and catalog XML ID returned no results; the current manuscript catalog supplies no public digital-surrogate link
access.format: No complete public page-image set found
notes: This is a dated negative search, not a claim that no internal digitization, preservation surrogate, or future public object exists.
Seton's fol. 48r plate is separately recorded and does not change the complete-surrogate status.

access.access_method: Register for Bodleian Archives & Manuscripts Requests, order MS. Bodl. 585 to Weston Library Level 1, and specify Part II and fols. 48r-101r; issue remains subject to access and condition
Request reference-quality PDF scans through Special Collections mediated copying; ask separately for the cataloged work fols. 48r-101r and for complete-codex coverage
access.format: Reference-quality PDF scans with shelfmark and folio metadata; higher-quality images available separately from Imaging Services
notes: For the complete host request, specify front and back covers, boards, spine, pastedowns, all end leaves and flyleaves, blanks, edges where feasible, and every manuscript leaf recto and verso; separately identify target fols. 48r-101r so the work is not confused with complete-codex scope.
Ask the repository to reconcile the catalog's fols. 48-103 physical part, inferred msItem endpoint 104, and text ending at fol. 101r before finalizing the imaging inventory.
The current service is free to Oxford members and otherwise costs £1 per scan, averages ten working days, and may decline items for legal, conservation, or other reasons.

rights.component: The medieval English and Latin texts, Seton's 1914 transcription and apparatus, and the published fol. 48r manuscript plate
rights.basis: The edition was first published in 1914, before the 1 January 1931 threshold stated in the April 2026 U.S. Copyright Office duration guidance; U-M also currently labels the item public domain in the United States.

rights.component: Materials presented in U-M CME item RuleMinoresses
rights.basis: The current item page expressly states that the materials are in the public domain and applies No Copyright-United States.
notes: The 1999 embedded availability notice permits personal, research, and teaching copies but forbids redistribution by commercial or not-for-profit content providers; whether the current statement supersedes that notice is unresolved.

rights.component: The local U-M-derived XML markup and transcription
rights.basis: The underlying edition and current U-M item are public domain in the United States, but the checked-in XML embeds an express redistribution restriction.

rights.component: Internet Archive's digital scan and generated derivatives
rights.basis: The scanned 1914 edition is public domain in the United States, but the reviewed Internet Archive item metadata supplies no item-specific rights, license, or licenseurl field for the scan derivatives.

rights.component: Modern photographs or scans supplied by Bodleian mediated copying or Imaging Services
rights.basis: No item-specific reusable image set or license was verified. Bodleian states that its terms apply whether underlying material is in copyright or public domain, permits non-commercial and academic uses under those terms, and requires separate permission for non-academic reproduction.
notes: Underlying medieval-text status and modern-image delivery or reuse terms are separate layers. Obtain and preserve the item-specific order terms with any supplied images.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Manuscript contractions and uncertain, altered, or supplied readings
editorial_practices.representation: Universally recognized contractions are expanded silently; editorial departures use italics, supplied letters or words are italicized in square brackets, Latin is italicized, and more elaborate Latin expansions are bracketed.
editorial_practices.description: Seton's note before the Franciscan texts states his expansion, italics, bracket, supply, and Latin conventions and expressly applies them to the Minoresses rule.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Composite host and Part II manuscript unit extending beyond the cataloged compilation
editorial_practices.representation: Seton transcribes the rule and Appendix through fol. 101r, describes the codex and later leaves in his introduction, and reproduces only fol. 48r as a manuscript plate.
editorial_practices.description: The printed component is a complete text edition of the cataloged compilation but only a selective visual presentation of the manuscript and no presentation of unrelated Part I.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Decorated or penwork initials at main-rule divisions and otherwise black manuscript writing
editorial_practices.representation: Monochrome print layout with paragraph spacing; a single monochrome plate preserves the visual opening at fol. 48r.
editorial_practices.description: Seton's description and the current catalog distinguish manuscript penwork from the edition's typographic presentation.
editorial_practices.scope: Main rule and selected fol. 48r plate

editorial_practices.source_feature: Seton's component title, introduction, bibliography, editorial note, plate, text, notes, and shared glossary
editorial_practices.representation: The electronic item retains the component title page and rule-plus-Appendix text; it omits the introduction, bibliography, editorial note, fol. 48r plate, endnotes, and glossary.
editorial_practices.description: The U-M item is a text-level selection from Seton's larger component and the composite EETS volume.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Seton's textual footnotes, corrected readings, deletions, unclear text, and supplied matter
editorial_practices.representation: Textual information from print footnotes is generally incorporated through CORR, DEL, UNCLEAR, and SUPPLIED tags; useful notes remain as NOTE1.
editorial_practices.description: The encoding converts most print-apparatus information into inline markup rather than preserving Seton's footnote layout.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Manuscript divisions marked by layout and decorated initials but not by the electronic labels Chapter 1-17 and Section 1-10
editorial_practices.representation: Twenty-seven DIV2 units receive SUPPLIED analytic headings; the two document boundaries within the Appendix are not expressed beyond section numbering.
editorial_practices.description: The electronic table of contents makes divisions searchable but must not be mistaken for a medieval numbered chapter system or a claim that all ten Appendix sections are one source document.
editorial_practices.scope: Seventeen main-rule divisions and ten Appendix divisions

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_1-2/RuleMinoresses.xml, header, editorial declaration, and full text.
evidence.summary: Supplies the 1999 U-M identity and production statement, 1914 source, erroneous inherited witness note, omissions, editorial tag conversion, embedded provider terms, folio and page milestones, and the complete rule-plus-Appendix text.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, CME item RuleMinoresses.
evidence.locator: Item information, rights statement, full-text action, and contents
evidence.summary: Confirms Seton editorship, 1914 London publication, public full text, stable handle, seventeen chapters plus ten Appendix sections, and current Public Domain / No Copyright-United States statement.

evidence.citation: R. W. Chambers and Walter W. Seton, eds., A Fifteenth-Century Courtesy Book and Two Fifteenth-Century Franciscan Rules, EETS OS 148 (1914), Internet Archive courtesybook00unknuoft.
evidence.locator: Title page and contents; Seton pp. 61-78, component title and editorial note, fol. 48r plate, text pp. 81-116, notes pp. 117-125, glossary pp. 126-127
evidence.summary: The inspected complete scan establishes edition roles and bibliography, source-rule history, Bodley manuscript structure and proposed provenance, Appendix source boundary, translation assessment, editorial conventions, direct transcription, one selected manuscript plate, notes, and glossary.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive item metadata for courtesybook00unknuoft.
evidence.summary: Supplies the University of Toronto Kelly Library provenance, scanner uoft1, exact PDF filename and size, derivative inventory, and absence of item-specific rights, license, or licenseurl fields.

evidence.citation: Direct inspection and page mapping of Seton's Minoresses component in the Internet Archive scan.
evidence.locator: PDF p. 98 / IA n97 (fol. 48r plate); PDF pp. 99-134 / IA n98-n133 (printed pp. 81-116)
evidence.summary: Verifies the plate caption and manuscript page as fol. 48r, confirms that it is the sole Bodley witness image in the component, and maps the full cataloged text from printed pp. 81-116 to exact PDF and BookReader pages.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Western Medieval Manuscripts, MS. Bodl. 585, pinned catalog TEI at medieval-mss commit cd36dce9a104617f960dab2c97ea78f95f917496.
evidence.locator: msDesc MS_Bodl_585; msPart MS_Bodl_585-part2; msItem MS_Bodl_585-part2-item1
evidence.summary: Confirms current shelfmark and identifiers, composite codex structure, Part II's Middle English Rule of St Clare item and IPMEP number, parchment and penwork, c. 1440-1460 English origin, Charles Howard's donation, tentative London-house association, and the record's evidence history.
notes: The human catalog page used an anti-bot interstitial during automated review; claims were verified in the exact pinned official TEI source.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian Data API exact searches for MS. Bodl. 585, performed 15 July 2026.
evidence.summary: Exact JSON-LD API queries by shelfmark, title, and catalog XML ID each returned totalItems 0. Together with the current catalog's lack of a digital-surrogate link, this supports a dated no-public-complete-copy finding, not an assertion that no internal images exist.
notes: Queries were made with Accept: application/ld+json as specified by the official API documentation.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Bodleian Archives & Manuscripts Requests.
evidence.summary: States that registered readers may request Western medieval manuscripts through the online service for consultation in Weston Library Level 1, subject to item access and restrictions.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Special collections mediated copying.
evidence.summary: Offers reference-quality PDF scans to all requesters, gives the order route and contact, states exceptions for legal or conservation reasons, reports £1 per scan for non-Oxford requesters and an average ten-working-day turnaround, and points to Imaging Services terms.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Reproducing images from Imaging Services.
evidence.summary: States that supplied images are governed by Bodleian terms whether the underlying material is in copyright or public domain, permits non-commercial and academic uses under those terms, and requires separate permission for non-academic reproduction.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright, revised April 2026.
evidence.locator: p. 2, works already under statutory protection before 1978
evidence.summary: States that U.S.-published works before 1 January 1931 are public domain and describes the duration framework supporting the assessment for the 1914 edition.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_1-2/3rdFranRule.xml, inspected solely to resolve the RuleMinoresses header's inherited witness note.
evidence.locator: HEADER SOURCEDESC; FRONT title page; folio milestones
evidence.summary: Shows that 'olim Seton, olim Pennant' is the source-witness designation for the sibling Third Order rule. Seton's separate title pages and the existing 3rdFranRule lineage identify that witness as modern NLW MS 22873B, excluding it from RuleMinoresses.

open_questions.question: Does U-M's current No Copyright-United States statement supersede the 1999 XML restriction against redistribution by commercial or not-for-profit content providers?
next_steps: Obtain a written interpretation from U-M before redistributing the XML or a close derivative.
Continue to distinguish copyright status from provider-contract status.

open_questions.question: Does Bodleian offer an existing complete digital, preservation, or microfilm surrogate of MS. Bodl. 585, and what item-specific access and reuse terms would apply?
next_steps: Ask Bodleian by shelfmark about internal surrogates, conservation limits, complete-codex and work-folio delivery scopes, formats, fees, and item-specific terms.
If ordering new images, request the complete physical host and fols. 48r-101r as separately named scopes and reconcile the 103/104 endpoint discrepancy.
Do not treat Seton's single fol. 48r plate or the complete printed-book scan as a complete manuscript facsimile.

open_questions.question: What exact documents underlie Appendix section 1 and sections 2-10, and where, when, and by whom were they translated, composed, and compiled with the main rule?
next_steps: Search current papal-register and Franciscan corpora for section 1 and compare sections 2-10 with Poor Clare and Friars Minor customaries and ordinals.
Do not add a directed source edge until an exact textual source or securely demonstrated analogue is found.

open_questions.question: How should the Part II physical endpoint and the content after the fol. 101r trailer be reconciled across the current catalog and Seton's description?
next_steps: Inspect or obtain images of fols. 101v through the end of the current part and all end leaves.
Ask Bodleian whether physical fols. 48-103, total leaf count 104, and inferred msItem endpoint 104 reflect blank leaves, cataloging convention, or a genuine discrepancy.

open_questions.question: Was MS. Bodl. 585 Part II copied for or used by the London Minoresses outside Aldgate?
next_steps: Preserve the current Bodleian/MLGB3 question mark and seek direct codicological, liturgical, obit, and archival evidence.
Review later provenance scholarship before upgrading Seton's contents-based conclusion or his inferred post-Dissolution ownership chain.

open_questions.question: Which exact French or Latin version served as the exemplar for the Middle English main rule?
next_steps: Collate the English text with surviving Isabella Rule recensions and French translations.
Retain Seton's French-intermediary judgment as probable, not as an identified source object.
